quizzes.explanation: Spaced repetition (reviewing material at increasing intervals) and active recall (testing yourself) are evidence-based study techniques proven to improve long-term retention far more than passive re-reading or cramming.

quizzes.q: CET passing rates depend on:
quizzes.explanation: Success on the CET is directly tied to preparation and practice. Consistent study, understanding of concepts, and familiarity with the test format are the most reliable predictors of a good score.

quizzes.q: Some CETs include a section on:
quizzes.explanation: Some CETs include a General Information section that tests awareness of current events, social issues, and general knowledge. Staying informed through news and reading helps with this section.

quizzes.q: What is the most effective review strategy?
quizzes.explanation: Active recall (testing yourself) and spaced repetition (reviewing material over time) are scientifically proven to be the most effective study methods, far superior to passive re-reading or cramming.

quizzes.q: How can you reduce test anxiety?
quizzes.explanation: Deep breathing activates the parasympathetic nervous system, which calms the body. Positive visualization helps create a mental rehearsal of success, both of which reduce anxiety.

quizzes.q: The night before the exam, the best preparation is:
quizzes.explanation: Adequate sleep (7-8 hours) is crucial before an exam. Sleep helps consolidate memory, and a well-rested brain performs significantly better in terms of focus, recall, and problem-solving than an exhausted one.

quizzes.q: To reduce test anxiety, you should:
quizzes.explanation: Deep breathing exercises activate the body's relaxation response, lowering heart rate and cortisol levels. This is a simple, effective technique you can use even during the exam itself.

quizzes.q: What should you bring on exam day?
opts: Permitted materials: pencils, ID, admission slip
quizzes.explanation: Always bring the required and permitted materials: sharpened pencils (No. 2), valid ID, and your admission slip or test permit. Bringing unauthorized materials like textbooks is not allowed and could result in disqualification.

quizzes.q: Eating a balanced breakfast before the exam helps because:
quizzes.explanation: A balanced breakfast with protein and complex carbohydrates provides sustained energy and stable blood sugar, which supports focus and cognitive function throughout the exam.

quizzes.q: The night before an exam, you should:
quizzes.explanation: Getting adequate sleep the night before an exam is more beneficial than last-minute studying. Sleep consolidates memories and ensures your brain is rested and ready to perform.

quizzes.q: Deep breathing helps with exam stress by:
quizzes.explanation: Deep, slow breathing stimulates the vagus nerve and activates the parasympathetic nervous system, which counteracts the stress response and brings a sense of calm.

quizzes.q: A healthy breakfast before an exam should include:
quizzes.explanation: Protein and complex carbohydrates (like eggs, oatmeal, or whole-grain bread) provide steady, sustained energy. Simple sugars cause energy spikes and crashes, while coffee alone doesn't provide nutrition.

quizzes.q: Positive self-talk before an exam means:
quizzes.explanation: Positive self-talk involves affirming your preparation and ability. Statements like 'I have studied hard and I am ready' help build confidence and reduce anxiety, improving performance.

quizzes.q: Arriving early to the exam venue helps:
quizzes.explanation: Arriving early gives you time to find your seat, use the restroom, organize your materials, and mentally prepare. Rushing in late increases stress and can negatively impact your focus.

quizzes.q: During an exam, if you feel anxious:
opts: Take a brief pause and breathe
quizzes.explanation: Taking a brief pause to breathe when anxious during an exam helps reset your nervous system. Even 30 seconds of focused breathing can restore calm and improve your ability to think clearly.

quizzes.q: Cramming the morning of the exam:
quizzes.explanation: Morning-of cramming can overload your brain with information, increase anxiety, and cause confusion between similar concepts. A calm review of key points is better than frantic last-minute studying.

quizzes.q: Physical exercise before an exam:
quizzes.explanation: Light exercise such as a short walk or gentle stretching releases endorphins and reduces stress hormones, helping you feel more alert and relaxed before the exam.

quizzes.q: Staying hydrated during an exam:
quizzes.explanation: Even mild dehydration can impair cognitive function, concentration, and memory. Drinking water before and during the exam (if allowed) helps maintain optimal brain performance.

quizzes.q: If you feel stuck on a question:
opts: Skip it and come back later
Give up on the whole test
quizzes.explanation: Skipping a difficult question and returning to it later is a smart strategy. It prevents wasted time and anxiety, and sometimes later questions can trigger your memory or understanding of earlier ones.

quizzes.q: A positive exam mindset includes:
quizzes.explanation: Believing in your preparation means trusting that your study efforts have equipped you well. This balanced confidence — not overconfidence — helps you stay calm, focused, and perform at your best.

quizzes.q: The best way to handle test anxiety is:
opts: Prepare well and use relaxation techniques
quizzes.explanation: The combination of thorough preparation (which builds genuine confidence) and relaxation techniques (deep breathing, visualization, positive self-talk) is the most effective and healthy way to manage test anxiety.

quizzes.q: What should you bring to the CET exam?
quizzes.explanation: Bring only permitted materials: sharpened pencils, eraser, valid ID, and admission slip. Some CETs allow a basic calculator. Phones, textbooks, and food are generally not allowed inside the testing room.

quizzes.q: For math problems, a useful first step is:
opts: Identify what is given and what is asked
quizzes.explanation: Identifying what is given (known values) and what is asked (the unknown) is the foundation of problem-solving. This helps you choose the right formula or method and prevents careless errors.

quizzes.q: For reading comprehension passages, you should:
opts: Read questions first, then scan passage
quizzes.explanation: Reading the questions first tells you what information to look for. You can then scan the passage strategically for relevant details, saving time and improving accuracy.

quizzes.q: In the abstract reasoning section, look for:
quizzes.explanation: Abstract reasoning tests your ability to identify visual patterns, rotations, progressions, and relationships among shapes. Look for changes in size, position, shading, number of sides, or symmetry.

quizzes.q: For math sections, the best approach is:
opts: Show your work and double-check calculations
quizzes.explanation: Showing your work helps you track your reasoning and catch errors. Double-checking calculations — especially unit conversions and arithmetic — prevents simple mistakes that cost points.

quizzes.q: For reading comprehension, you should:
opts: Read the questions first, then the passage
Read the passage first, then questions
quizzes.explanation: Reading the questions first gives you a purpose for reading. You know what to look for, which makes your reading more focused and efficient than reading the entire passage without knowing what's being asked.

quizzes.q: In vocabulary questions, context clues help by:
quizzes.explanation: Context clues are words or phrases near an unfamiliar word that hint at its meaning. Synonyms, antonyms, examples, and explanations in surrounding text can help you infer the correct definition.

quizzes.q: For science questions, the key is:
quizzes.explanation: Understanding scientific concepts and how to apply them is more effective than rote memorization. Many science questions test your ability to apply principles to new situations, not just recall facts.

quizzes.q: Filipino section tips include:
quizzes.explanation: Success in the Filipino section depends on understanding context, proper grammar usage, and Filipino vocabulary. Translating to English can cause errors since direct translations don't always capture the correct meaning.

quizzes.q: For abstract reasoning, practice helps by:
quizzes.explanation: Practice trains your brain to recognize common pattern types (rotation, progression, mirroring, etc.) more quickly. Speed in pattern recognition is critical since abstract reasoning sections are often timed.

quizzes.q: In essay sections, planning before writing:
quizzes.explanation: Taking a few minutes to outline your essay ensures a logical structure with a clear introduction, supporting points, and conclusion. This leads to a more coherent and higher-scoring response.

quizzes.q: For general information sections:
quizzes.explanation: General information sections test awareness of current events, government, geography, and social issues. Regularly reading news and staying informed gives you a strong foundation for this section.

quizzes.q: When unsure between two math answers:
quizzes.explanation: Re-reading the problem and re-calculating helps catch common errors like misreading the question, using the wrong formula, or making arithmetic mistakes. A second look often reveals the correct answer.

quizzes.q: For grammar questions, the best strategy is:
quizzes.explanation: While 'what sounds right' sometimes works for native speakers, applying grammar rules systematically (subject-verb agreement, tense consistency, pronoun reference) is more reliable, especially for tricky questions.

quizzes.q: The most important thing for CET success is:
quizzes.explanation: Consistent preparation over time — studying concepts, practicing problems, and taking mock exams — is the single most important factor for CET success. It builds both knowledge and confidence.

quizzes.q: For analogy questions, the relationship is:
opts: Systematic - find the pattern between word pairs
quizzes.explanation: Analogy questions test your ability to identify the relationship between a pair of words and find another pair with the same relationship. Common relationships include synonym, antonym, part-to-whole, cause-and-effect, and category.

quizzes.q: STEM stands for:
quizzes.explanation: STEM is an acronym for Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics. It represents a grouping of academic disciplines that are critical for innovation and competitiveness in the modern economy.

quizzes.q: ABM strand focuses on:
quizzes.explanation: ABM stands for Accountancy, Business, and Management. This strand prepares students for careers in business, finance, accounting, and entrepreneurship by teaching foundational concepts in these areas.

quizzes.q: HUMSS stands for:
quizzes.explanation: HUMSS stands for Humanities and Social Sciences. This academic track focuses on understanding human behavior, society, culture, and social issues through subjects like history, philosophy, political science, and sociology.

quizzes.q: GAS strand is for students who:
opts: Are undecided on their career path
quizzes.explanation: GAS stands for General Academic Strand. It is designed for students who are still undecided about their career path, offering a mix of subjects from different tracks to provide broad academic preparation.

quizzes.q: How many parts does it have?
quizzes.explanation: The CET (College Entrance Test) typically has 3 parts covering different subject areas such as language proficiency, mathematics, and general knowledge. This structure allows for comprehensive assessment of student readiness.

quizzes.q: STEM strand focuses on:
quizzes.explanation: The STEM strand in Senior High School focuses on Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics. It develops analytical and problem-solving skills essential for careers in scientific and technical fields.

quizzes.q: ABM strand prepares students for:
quizzes.explanation: ABM prepares students for business and accounting careers by teaching subjects like financial management, business ethics, economics, and accounting principles. Graduates often pursue degrees in business administration, accountancy, or related fields.

quizzes.q: HUMSS strand focuses on:
quizzes.explanation: HUMSS focuses on social sciences and humanities, covering subjects like literature, history, philosophy, political science, and sociology. It develops critical thinking and communication skills for careers in law, education, and social work.

quizzes.q: The GAS strand in SHS stands for:
quizzes.explanation: GAS stands for General Academic Strand. Unlike specialized tracks, GAS provides a well-rounded curriculum that draws from various academic disciplines, making it ideal for students exploring different career options.

quizzes.q: GAS stands for:
quizzes.explanation: GAS stands for General Academic Strand, one of the academic tracks in the Senior High School curriculum under the K-12 program in the Philippines. It offers a flexible curriculum combining subjects from different strands.

quizzes.q: Which career is most aligned with STEM?
quizzes.explanation: Engineering is most aligned with STEM because it directly applies principles of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics to solve real-world problems. While other careers may use some STEM skills, engineering is fundamentally built on these disciplines.

quizzes.q: ABM students typically study:
quizzes.explanation: ABM students study accounting, business, and economics subjects. These courses provide the foundation for understanding financial management, business operations, market economics, and entrepreneurial concepts.

quizzes.q: HUMSS students are prepared for careers in:
quizzes.explanation: HUMSS students develop skills in communication, critical thinking, and understanding human society. This prepares them for careers in law, social work, education, journalism, public administration, and other fields that require strong interpersonal and analytical skills.

quizzes.q: GAS strand is ideal for students who:
opts: Know exactly what career they want
quizzes.explanation: GAS is ideal for students who are undecided about their specialization. It provides exposure to various academic disciplines, helping students discover their interests before committing to a specific college course or career path.

quizzes.q: In STEM, research and innovation focus on:
quizzes.explanation: STEM education emphasizes scientific inquiry and problem-solving. Students learn to use the scientific method, conduct research, analyze data, and develop innovative solutions to complex problems using technology and engineering principles.

quizzes.q: ABM prepares students for:
quizzes.explanation: ABM prepares students for business and entrepreneurship by teaching financial literacy, management principles, and business operations. Students gain skills applicable to starting their own businesses or working in corporate environments.

quizzes.q: The Senior High School program in the Philippines has:
quizzes.explanation: Under the K-12 program implemented in the Philippines, Senior High School (SHS) consists of 2 years (Grades 11 and 12). This was added to the previous 10-year basic education system to align with international standards.

quizzes.q: Which is NOT a senior high school strand?
quizzes.explanation: MEDICAL is not an official senior high school strand. The recognized academic strands are STEM, ABM, HUMSS, and GAS. While there are science subjects in STEM, there is no dedicated 'Medical' strand at the SHS level.

quizzes.q: Which is NOT a TVL track?
quizzes.explanation: ABM (Accountancy, Business, and Management) is not a TVL track—it is a separate academic strand. The TVL tracks include Home Economics (HE), ICT, Industrial Arts, and Agri-Fishery. ABM focuses on business education rather than technical-vocational skills.

quizzes.q: TVET stands for:
quizzes.explanation: TVET stands for Technical-Vocational Education and Training. It is a comprehensive term used internationally to describe education and training programs that equip learners with practical skills and knowledge for specific occupations or trades.

quizzes.q: The Sports Track in senior high prepares students for careers in:
quizzes.explanation: The Sports Track prepares students for careers in athletics, coaching, and sports management. Students learn sports science, physical fitness, coaching techniques, and sports administration to enter the growing sports industry.

quizzes.q: The Arts and Design Track focuses on:
quizzes.explanation: The Arts and Design Track focuses on creative and artistic disciplines. Students explore visual arts, media arts, performing arts, and creative industries, developing skills in artistic expression, design thinking, and cultural appreciation.

quizzes.q: The Arts and Design track focuses on:
quizzes.explanation: The Arts and Design track emphasizes creative expression and artistic skills. Students develop their artistic abilities through various media and techniques, preparing them for careers in creative industries or further studies in arts-related fields.

quizzes.q: Visual arts include:
quizzes.explanation: Visual arts encompass a wide range of creative expressions including painting, sculpture, photography, printmaking, ceramics, and digital art. It is not limited to just one medium—it is a broad field of artistic creation meant to be visually perceived.

quizzes.q: The elements of art include:
opts: Line, shape, color, texture, and more
quizzes.explanation: The elements of art are the fundamental building blocks of visual art: line, shape, color, texture, value, form, and space. Artists combine these elements to create compositions and convey meaning in their work.

quizzes.q: Principles of design include:
quizzes.explanation: The principles of design are guidelines for organizing the elements of art. They include balance, contrast, emphasis, movement, pattern, rhythm, and unity/variety. These principles help artists create effective and aesthetically pleasing compositions.

quizzes.q: Which is NOT a visual art form?
quizzes.explanation: Accounting is not a visual art form. It is a business and financial discipline. Painting, sculpture, and photography are all recognized visual art forms that involve creative expression through visual media.

quizzes.q: The color wheel was developed by:
quizzes.explanation: Sir Isaac Newton developed the first color wheel in 1666. He created it by splitting white light through a prism to reveal the spectrum of colors, then arranged them in a circular format. This became foundational for color theory in art and science.

quizzes.q: Primary colors are:
quizzes.explanation: In traditional color theory, the primary colors are red, blue, and yellow. These colors cannot be created by mixing other colors and serve as the foundation for creating all other colors through mixing. (In light/RGB, they are red, green, and blue.)

quizzes.q: Secondary colors are made by:
quizzes.explanation: Secondary colors are created by mixing two primary colors together. For example: red + blue = purple, red + yellow = orange, and blue + yellow = green. This principle is fundamental to color theory in art.

quizzes.q: Complementary colors are:
opts: Next to each other on the wheel
quizzes.explanation: Complementary colors are positioned opposite each other on the color wheel (e.g., red and green, blue and orange, yellow and purple). When placed side by side, they create maximum contrast and visual vibrancy. When mixed, they neutralize each other.

quizzes.q: In art, 'perspective' refers to:
quizzes.explanation: In art, perspective refers to the technique of creating depth and dimension on a flat surface. It uses principles like vanishing points, foreshortening, and atmospheric perspective to make two-dimensional artwork appear three-dimensional.

quizzes.q: A career in arts and design includes:
opts: Graphic design, animation, interior design, and more
quizzes.explanation: Arts and design careers are diverse and include graphic design, animation, interior design, fashion design, illustration, web design, film production, and more. The creative industries offer numerous paths for those with artistic skills and training.

quizzes.q: Digital art includes:
quizzes.explanation: Digital art encompasses computer-generated images, animation, digital painting, 3D modeling, graphic design, and other artworks created using digital technology. It has become a major artistic medium with applications in entertainment, advertising, and fine art.

quizzes.q: An engineering program typically requires strong skills in:
quizzes.explanation: Engineering programs demand strong mathematics and science skills because engineers apply mathematical principles and scientific knowledge to design, build, and maintain structures, machines, and systems. Calculus, physics, and chemistry are fundamental requirements.

quizzes.q: A BSN program prepares students to become:
quizzes.explanation: BSN stands for Bachelor of Science in Nursing. This program prepares students to become registered nurses by teaching patient care, medical procedures, health assessment, and nursing theory, along with clinical practice.

quizzes.q: Which college program focuses on financial management and auditing?
quizzes.explanation: BSBA (Bachelor of Science in Business Administration) and BSA (Bachelor of Science in Accountancy) focus on financial management and auditing. These programs teach accounting principles, financial analysis, taxation, and business management.

quizzes.q: TVL stands for:
quizzes.explanation: TVL stands for Technical-Vocational-Livelihood. It is one of the tracks in Senior High School that provides students with practical, job-ready skills in various technical and vocational fields for immediate employment or entrepreneurship.

quizzes.q: TVL strand prepares students for:
quizzes.explanation: TVL prepares students for immediate employment or entrepreneurship after graduation. Unlike academic tracks that primarily prepare students for college, TVL equips students with hands-on, marketable skills they can use right away.

quizzes.q: Which is a TVL specialization?
quizzes.explanation: Home Economics is one of the specializations under the TVL track. It covers areas like cookery, food and beverage services, tourism, dressmaking, and other livelihood skills. Other TVL specializations include ICT, Industrial Arts, and Agri-Fishery.

quizzes.q: The Sports strand focuses on:
opts: Physical education, sports science, and coaching
quizzes.explanation: The Sports strand focuses on physical education, sports science, and coaching. Students learn about exercise physiology, sports psychology, coaching methods, fitness training, and sports management to prepare for careers in the sports industry.

quizzes.q: NC stands for in TVL context:
quizzes.explanation: NC stands for National Certificate, issued by TESDA (Technical Education and Skills Development Authority). It certifies that a person has achieved competency in specific technical skills through assessment, which is valuable for employment both locally and abroad.

quizzes.q: TVL graduates can:
quizzes.explanation: TVL graduates can take TESDA assessments to earn National Certificates (NC). These certifications validate their technical skills and make them more employable. They can also pursue college education or start their own businesses.

quizzes.q: The Sports strand includes training in:
quizzes.explanation: The Sports strand provides comprehensive training in sports management, coaching, and fitness, not just playing sports. Students learn about sports administration, event management, athletic training, nutrition, and leadership in sports contexts.

quizzes.q: TESDA is the agency that:
quizzes.explanation: TESDA (Technical Education and Skills Development Authority) provides technical-vocational training and assessment in the Philippines. It develops curriculum, registers training programs, and conducts competency assessments for issuing National Certificates.

quizzes.q: A TVL student can specialize in:
opts: Home Economics, ICT, Industrial Arts, and more
quizzes.explanation: TVL offers multiple specializations including Home Economics (HE), Information and Communications Technology (ICT), Industrial Arts (IA), and Agri-Fishery Arts. Each specialization has various specific courses students can choose from.

quizzes.q: Sports strand graduates can become:
quizzes.explanation: Sports strand graduates have diverse career options beyond just being athletes. They can become coaches, fitness trainers, sports managers, recreation coordinators, sports event organizers, or pursue further studies in sports science.

quizzes.q: The advantage of TVL is:
quizzes.explanation: The main advantage of TVL is that it provides hands-on, practical skills for employment. Students gain real-world experience and industry-relevant competencies that make them job-ready upon graduation, and they can earn TESDA certifications.

quizzes.q: Which is NOT a TVL track?
quizzes.explanation: Pre-Medicine is not a TVL track. The recognized TVL specializations are Home Economics, ICT, Industrial Arts, and Agri-Fishery Arts. Pre-Medicine is more aligned with the STEM academic track at the college level.
